duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Pre-existing BlueField row was nvidia-bluefield-3-dpu only. Parent-18 adds nvidia-bluefield-4-dpu as a distinct DPU SKU and leaves nvidia-bluefield-3-dpu unchanged.

Pre-existing BlueField observation was obs-bluefield-3-network-bandwidth with value 400 Gb/s. Parent-18 adds obs-bluefield-4-network-bandwidth with value 800 Gb/s and does not edit the BlueField-3 observation.

Pre-existing BlueField resolved spec was nvidia-bluefield-3-dpu network_bandwidth=400 Gb/s. Parent-18 adds nvidia-bluefield-4-dpu network_bandwidth=800 Gb/s and does not edit the BlueField-3 resolved field.

BlueField-4 appears as a separate DPU row. BlueField-4 STX, CMX/context-memory storage, storage throughput, cybersecurity, threat-detection, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, ecosystem, memory, host-interface, and compute text is explicitly boundary-only.

BlueField-4/CMX/STX is recorded only as AI-factory networking, context-tier, storage, and security adjacency. It is not promoted as protocol-level storage-fabric behavior, storage benchmark, production operation, or SLO evidence.

BlueField-4 security wording is recorded only as platform-boundary context; no tenant-policy correctness, threat-detection performance, compliance, incident-readiness, or service-SLO claim is promoted.
Parent-18 records BlueField-4 evidence while preserving the committed July 9 SGLang batch and parent-17 AWS Trn2 evidence as current duplicate-boundary state.

gap_reason: The local NVIDIA BlueField platform capture has a source-visible, product-specific BlueField-4 DPU portfolio entry stating that NVIDIA BlueField-4 is an 800Gb/s infrastructure platform for gigascale AI factories. The structured catalog previously represented only NVIDIA BlueField-3 DPU network_bandwidth=400 Gb/s and had no BlueField-4 DPU SKU, observation, or resolved row. The safe schema-supported promotion is therefore only BlueField-4 DPU network_bandwidth=800 Gb/s.

schema_boundary.source_value: The page states that NVIDIA BlueField-4 DPU is an 800Gb/s infrastructure platform for gigascale AI factories.
schema_boundary.boundary: The catalog resolves only the schema-supported DPU network_bandwidth field as 800 Gb/s.

schema_boundary.source_value: The page states BlueField-4 delivers six times the compute of its predecessor and has accelerations for networking, data storage, and cybersecurity.
schema_boundary.boundary: No compute throughput, storage throughput, memory, host-interface, power, or cybersecurity performance field is resolved because the local page does not expose schema-supported product-bound values for those fields.

schema_boundary.source_value: The page discusses BlueField-4 STX, Vera CPU, NVIDIA CMX context-memory storage, zero-trust file access, 1,000x faster threat detection, AI Factory Validated Design, ecosystem, rapid data access, high-performance inference, NVMe-oF, GPUDirect Storage, block/file/object storage, and runtime security.
schema_boundary.boundary: These items remain curated boundary evidence only. They are not structured catalog facts, protocol-level storage-fabric proof, benchmark results, production-operation evidence, SLO evidence, validated-design completion proof, or product ranking.

non_promoted_boundaries: No BlueField-3 structured row was changed.

No BlueField-4 STX, Vera CPU, CMX/context-memory storage, storage throughput, NVMe-oF, GPUDirect Storage, block/file/object storage, rapid data access, high-performance inference, cybersecurity, threat-detection, zero-trust file access, validated-design, ecosystem, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, power-efficiency, memory, host-interface, or compute field was promoted.

No SGLang July 9 scheduled crawler batch, AWS Trn2, JaguarMicro, Atlas 300I A2, MetaX, Iluvatar, Kunlunxin, Asterfusion, Yusur, Resnics, Corigine, Cambricon, H200, Kubernetes/Kueue/Volcano, data/RAG, KServe, MLCommons, or incident/postmortem rows were modified except duplicate-boundary context.
